impact_on_program.A_SP: Type D multipads: M_pad≤2 deployed. Type S (shared-root, free_core≥2) is the live multipad gap.
impact_on_program.next: Pay or kill Type S multipads (shared-root first-match / free_core reduction); high tag κ→[K_max] for sides
impact_on_program.residual: Residual multipad t=1 is VACUOUS — residual has no multipads after A_SP payment (R_sing only)

OPEN.name: OPEN_type_S_Mpad_and_high_tag
OPEN.statement:
(1) Bound Type S multipads at free_core=846161: shared-root A_SP multipads with t≥2 — e.g. reduce free_core along common roots, or first-match shared-root cell.
(2) Residual/A_SP free-1 highs ↪ [2176] for (κ,ι,δ) side marks.
Residual multipad t=1 is settled vacuously (no residual multipads).

payment.name: payment_path_after_residual_emptiness
payment.program: Kill or pay Type S multipads at free_core≫1; compress residual highs to [2176] for side marks under M_pad≤1 or ≤2.
proof: Residual emptiness (lemma 1) + Type D packing + v20 N_ord bound.
payment.statement: First-match residual mass after paying A_SP is pure R_sing (no multipad). A_SP payment still needs N_ord ≤ M_pad · N_side on A_SP multipads, with Type D contributing M_pad ≤ 2 deployed and Type S open. Side injection (ι,δ)+high tag (v25–v28) remains the other half.

residual_empty.consequence: The slogan 'residual multipad t=1' is vacuously true: residual multipads do not exist. The live M_pad problem is entirely inside the A_SP payment cell.
residual_empty.name: residual_multipad_emptiness_after_A_SP
proof: Multipad side key (high,c0U,c0V) is populated only from ordered CS pairs (U,V) drawn from a free-1 pencil of size ≥2 on a common core (top-seam normal form v15–v20).
Multi-member free-1 pencils are exactly the A_SP assignment set (v17).
R_sing := Fib \ A_SP has only singleton pencils ⇒ no CS ordered pair of that form ⇒ no multipad side key ⇒ M_pad:=1, N_ord=0.
First-match form (v17): pay A_SP cell; residual ⊆ R_sing.
residual_empty.statement: Under the first-match fiber partition Fib_w(z) = A_SP(z) ⊔ R_sing(z) (v17), where A_SP is the set of j-supports in multi-member top-seam core pencils and R_sing is the complementary singleton-pencil residual: every multipad event is supported on a free-1 CS ordered pair arising from a multi-member pencil, hence only on A_SP. Consequently R_sing has N_ord=0 and M_pad=1 vacuously. After the A_SP first-match cell is paid, the residual carries no multipad term.

type_DS.name: A_SP_multipad_type_D_vs_S
type_DS.statement: For each A_SP multipad side key (U,V) with core set Cores, define t = max_r |{C ∈ Cores : r ∈ C}| over r ∈ D\(U∪V). Type D: t=1 (pairwise disjoint cores). Type S: t≥2 (shared-root multipad).

type_D_pack.name: type_D_Mpad_packing_deployed_2
type_D_pack.statement: Type D multipads have t=1, hence M_pad ≤ ⌊(n−2e)/m_c⌋ = 2 at deployed parameters (v28). free_core=1 multipads are always Type D (v27 |C∩C'|=0), so free_core=1 ⇒ M_pad ≤ 2 deployed as well.

type_S_fc.deployed_note: Deployed free_core=846161 ≥ 2, so Type S is not ruled out by degree; it is the remaining A_SP multipad gap.
type_S_fc.name: type_S_requires_free_core_ge_2
proof: Type S ⇒ t≥2 ⇒ some r in two cores ⇒ |C∩C'|≥1.
type_S_fc.statement: Type S multipads can occur only when free_core ≥ 2. Equivalently free_core=1 ⇒ no Type S.

title: Residual multipad emptiness + Type D/S A_SP multipad split
